estimated.note: Higher estimate includes unreported deaths in restricted areas
Slight variance due to delayed military casualty reports

parties.name: Iranian Civilians

estimated.note: Wide range: HRANA says 7,015; Iranian doctors network says 16,500-18,000; Iran International estimates up to 36,500. Trump cited 32,000 in State of the Union.

parties.arrestedNote: Mass arrests during Dec 2025–Jan 2026 protests. HRANA documented 41,800+ detained across 31 provinces.

estimated.note: Includes UAE (3), Kuwait (4+), Bahrain, other GCC nations. 112+ wounded in UAE alone.

parties.name: Iran

confirmed.source: Iranian Health Ministry (as of March 15 — 1,444 confirmed killed)

estimated.note: HRANA estimates 1,858+ killed as of March 15 — higher than government's 1,444 figure. Includes 180 at Minab girls' school, 4 oil workers. 65 schools and 32 medical facilities damaged. 10,000+ civilian sites damaged. 5,000+ targets struck (CENTCOM confirmed). 101 sailors missing from IRINS Dena. Internet at 4% — independent verification nearly impossible. ~40 officials killed including Armed Forces CoS Bagheri, IRGC commanders Salami, Rashid, Hajizadeh.

rumored.note: IDF Brig Gen Defrin claims 6,000+ IRGC killed and 15,000 wounded (March 15). HRANA documented 1,097 civilian deaths by Day 4 alone — extrapolation through Day 16 implies far higher toll. 200+ children among dead per WHO. HRANA precedent from 2025 Twelve-Day War: official figure was 610, HRANA later confirmed 1,190 (nearly double). Internet at 4% makes independent verification near-impossible. ~20,000 civilian buildings, 77 healthcare facilities, 65 schools damaged.

socialSources.claim: By Day 4 alone: 1,097 civilians killed (including 181 children), 5,402 injured. This was already 3x the government's claimed toll at that point
Satellite imagery of Bandar Abbas naval base shows 8 destroyed structures — Iran only confirmed 15 KIA there. Real toll likely 200+
Leaked hospital records from Isfahan show 1,200 admitted in 48 hours — officials claim only 89 casualties in the city
IRINS Dena sinking alone could account for 100+ dead — only 79 of 180 crew recovered. Iran lists them as 'missing'
Experts say actual death toll 'could be far greater as more details are released' — Iran's internet blackout prevents verification
IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed, 15,000 wounded, 70% of Iran's missile launchers destroyed, 2,000+ targets hit. Plans through Passover (3+ more weeks).

parties.injuredNote: Includes hospital admissions across 14+ provinces. 77 healthcare facilities damaged, straining capacity.
parties.missingNote: 101 sailors from IRINS Dena — only 79 of 180 crew recovered after ship sank in Gulf of Oman.
parties.displacedNote: 3.2M displaced across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz and border regions. UNHCR reports shelter capacity exceeded.

parties.name: Israel

estimated.note: 15 confirmed killed (Alma Center). 2 killed by cluster munition strike in central Israel (Day 14). 9 killed in Beit Shemesh (Day 2). 3,195 hospitalized per Israel Health Ministry. 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated air defenses.

rumored.note: Iranian state media claims significantly higher Israeli casualties; some OSINT analysts report additional unreported incidents

socialSources.claim: Multiple videos show direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media — possible casualties suppressed by censorship
IRGC claims 40+ Israelis killed across 9 missile salvos — IDF only confirming 12

parties.injuredNote: 3,195 hospitalized per Israel Health Ministry (March 15). Includes 50+ wounded in Zarzir missile strike. 11 Iranian cluster missiles penetrated air defenses.

parties.name: United States

estimated.note: 19 confirmed KIA. Includes Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, all 6 KC-135 crew killed in crash near Turaibil, Iraq (Mar 12-13), and 6 additional KC-135 crew identified Mar 15. ~150 wounded. 4th manned US aircraft lost.

rumored.note: IRGC claims 650+ US killed/wounded in first 2 days (largely debunked by Jerusalem Post). Ali Larijani claims US soldiers were captured — Pentagon explicitly denied. Iran state TV broadcast alleged captured US troops. Multiple IRGC claims later deleted after being disproven.

socialSources.claim: IRGC spokesperson Ali Mohammad Naini claimed 650 US troops killed or wounded in first 2 days, including 160 at US military HQ in Bahrain. Claims largely disproven by Jerusalem Post investigation.
Claims missile strikes on 3 US/British oil tankers, shutdown of US naval facilities in Kuwait, 'hundreds of American military casualties.' Multiple claims later deleted.
Claims Iran captured US soldiers. 'Americans claim they have been killed in action to cover up captures.' Trump administration explicitly denied any captures.
Sources in Bahrain report 12+ additional US wounded at 5th Fleet HQ not in official Pentagon count

parties.injuredNote: 108 returned to duty, 8 severely injured. Includes casualties from drone attacks, IED strikes, and KC-135 crash. Updated March 15.

parties.name: Lebanon

confirmed.source: Lebanese Health Ministry (March 15 update — 850 killed including 106+ children, 2,009 wounded)

estimated.source: Lebanese media and Hezbollah casualty estimates
estimated.note: Includes Hezbollah fighters. 830,000+ displaced. 826 killed confirmed by Lebanese Health Ministry March 14. 12 medics killed in Burj Qalaouiyah health center strike. 1,100+ Israeli strikes in Lebanon since Feb 28. Israel planning ground invasion south of Litani River.

rumored.note: Hezbollah fighter casualties are not separately broken out in Lebanese Health Ministry figures. IDF planning major ground invasion south of Litani River — expected to significantly increase casualties. 1,100+ Israeli strikes on Lebanon since Feb 28. Rescue teams unable to access some strike sites.

socialSources.claim: 12 medics killed in single Israeli strike on Burj Qalaouiyah health center — rescue teams unable to reach multiple other sites in southern Lebanon
Israel bombs central Beirut and targets civilian infrastructure — death toll rising as hospitals overwhelmed in southern Lebanon

parties.injuredNote: 2,009 wounded per Lebanese Health Ministry (March 14). 15+ healthcare workers killed. IDF ground operations in border areas ongoing.
parties.displacedNote: 830,000+ displaced across Lebanon. Israel planning ground invasion south of Litani River.

parties.name: Gulf States (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, etc.)

confirmed.source: Gulf state media reports / defense ministry statements

estimated.source: Estimated from missile impact reports across Gulf and Iraq
estimated.note: Iraq: 20 killed (Baghdad airport rockets wounded 5 more, Mar 15). UAE: 6 killed, 142 injured, 262 missiles detected (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted. Kuwait: 1 killed. Bahrain: 27 killed (+ 32 wounded), 125 ballistic missiles and 211 drones intercepted since war began. Saudi Arabia: 2 killed. Oman: 3 killed.

rumored.note: Potential unreported migrant worker deaths in UAE, Bahrain, and Oman not included in official counts. Several confirmed dead were foreign nationals. Air defense interception rates (92-95%) suggest toll could have been far higher if systems failed.

socialSources.claim: Woman killed in Bahrain as other Gulf states intercept Iranian missiles — multiple foreign nationals among casualties across Gulf
Iranian projectiles continue to strike Gulf countries' infrastructure — full casualty picture unclear as some nations restrict reporting

parties.injuredNote: UAE: 142 injured. Bahrain: 32 wounded. Iraq: 5+ wounded (Baghdad airport rockets, Mar 15). 262 missiles detected over UAE (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted.

confirmed.source: French Ministry of Armed Forces (March 12). WO Arnaud Frion, 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion, killed in drone strike near Makhmour, Iraq. First non-US NATO fatality.

estimated.note: WO Arnaud Frion killed at French base near Makhmour, Iraq. 6 French soldiers total wounded. Macron condemned attack.
